subject: DevOps
title: Message Queues and Event Streaming
description: A micro-lesson that makes Message Queues and Event Streaming usable.

hook.text: The payment service crashed. Without a queue, 10,000 orders vanished. With one, they'd have waited.

buildup.text: Queues decouple services. The sender drops a message and moves on. The receiver processes it later.

discovery.text: SQS and RabbitMQ are queues. Kafka and Kinesis stream high-throughput, ordered event data.

twist.text: Queues absorb spikes. If 1,000 requests hit but you process 100/sec, the queue buffers the rest.

climax.text: Event-driven systems use queues as backbone. Services talk via events, not direct API calls.

punchline.text: Queues decouple, buffer, and protect. Essential cloud plumbing.

quiz.question: How do message queues help during traffic spikes?
options: They buffer excess messages until the receiver can process them
They automatically scale the receiving service
They drop messages that exceed the queue limit
